overview: Esper Murktide is a tempo strategy that leverages cheap interaction and card advantage to control the early game. By deploying Psychic Frog alongside efficient removal and counterspells, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents before they can establish a dominant board state. The win condition revolves around maintaining board presence while slowly chipping away at the opponent's life total or outlasting them in a resource war.
win_conditions: Trading up with Psychic Frog to draw into finishers
Countering key threats to gain tempo advantage
Using Fatal Push to clear the path for aggressive Frog attacks

keep_criteria: Hands containing Psychic Frog or Fatal Push
Hands with at least one counterspell and a land
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or creatures
Hands with three or more lands
Hands without Psychic Frog or Fatal Push

early_game: Play Psychic Frog on turn one to establish presence
Use Fatal Push to remove early aggressive threats
Hold Counterspell for opponent's key plays
mid_game: Trade Psychic Frog repeatedly to draw cards
Maintain board control with efficient removal
Apply steady pressure while waiting for opponent's resources to dry up
late_game: Close out with accumulated card advantage
Use Fatal Push to clear the final path to victory

key_cards.name: Psychic Frog
key_cards.role: Early board presence and card engine
key_cards.notes: Trade it early to draw cards and fuel your hand, ensuring you stay ahead in resources

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Hold for opponent's key threats or combo pieces to gain tempo and control the board state

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Use on low-toughness threats to clear the way for Frog attacks or protect your board | Used to efficiently remove a 3/3 flying Dragon's Rage Channeler to clear the way for attacks, highlighting its value as cheap, targeted removal in this matchup. (from gameplay)

sequencing_tips: Hold Counterspell for the opponent's most impactful spell rather than using it preemptively
Trade Psychic Frog early to draw into Fatal Push or additional interaction
Use Fatal Push on threats that would otherwise trade with Frog to maximize card advantage
Save reactive removal like Spell Snare for after Teferi enters the battlefield to protect it, while using proactive removal like Unholy Heat on early threats like Felia to avoid wasting interaction.

When facing a haste creature with Teferi on the board, delay casting Subtlety until the attack step to safely block and bounce the creature, preventing the opponent from playing spells in response due to Teferi's static ability.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Fatal Push on early threats and hold Counterspell for their key finishers
Use Frog to draw into answers and apply steady pressure while waiting for their resources to dry up
Save Counterspell for their win condition and use Fatal Push to disrupt their setup

Sun Cleanser is highly effective against energy decks, as it removes existing energy counters and prevents the opponent from gaining new ones. (from gameplay) Delighted Halfling can help resolve Nadu through Counterspell, but it is ineffective against Subtlety in games one and two before the sideboard. (from gameplay) Use Force of Negation to counter Cordial Calling, and rely on Solitude to deal with Nadu while it is still on the stack. (from gameplay)

Fatal Push is a critical clean answer to Blink creatures like Solitude, Ephemerate, and Felia. This specific opponent list lacks Solitude, making cheap removal even more impactful for controlling the early board. (from gameplay) Be wary of Subtlety bouncing your Prowess creatures back to the top of your library. If you are mana-screwed, the bounce can severely disrupt your game plan by putting a non-land on top when you desperately need mana. (from gameplay)
